primitive-types: Rust primitive types (integer, float, boolean, char) and type inference rules
variables-immutability: Rust variable declarations, constants, mutability restrictions, and variable shadowing
data-structures: Rust data structures: arrays, vectors, tuples, and hash maps usage
module-system: Rust module system: use statements, pub visibility, and crate organization
ownership: Rust ownership rules, stack vs heap memory, and move semantics
borrowing: Rust borrowing system: immutable and mutable references with borrow checker
slices: Rust slices, string slices, and slicing operations on arrays and vectors
lifetimes: Rust lifetime annotations, borrow checker rules, and reference validity
results: Rust Result<T, E> type, error variants, and error propagation patterns
options: Rust Option<T> type, Some/None variants, and handling absent values
error-handling: Rust error handling: question mark operator, unwrap(), match, and if let
custom-errors: Rust custom error types, the Error trait, and error conversion
functions: Rust function declarations, method signatures, associated functions, and self keyword
structs: Rust struct definitions, instances, tuple structs, and unit structs
enums: Rust enum definitions, variants, pattern matching, and if let expressions
traits: Rust trait definitions, impl blocks, trait bounds, and default implementations
generics: Rust generic types, functions, methods, and constraint syntax usage
lifetimes-advanced: Rust lifetime elision rules, generic lifetimes, and lifetime annotations
concurrency: Rust concurrency: threads, Send and Sync traits, and channel communication
smart-pointers: Rust smart pointers: Box, Rc, Arc, Cell, and RefCell custom implementations
macros: Rust declarative macros with macro_rules!, derive macros, and metaprogramming
